c0.q: Who gives out the prize named after the author of Lectures on Jurisprudence?
c0.gen_cached:
The prize named after the author of *Lectures on Jurisprudence*—Adam Smith—is given out by the **University of Cambridge**.

Specifically, the **Adam Smith Prizes** are awarded annually by the University of Cambridge for:
- The best overall performance in Part IIB of the Economics Tripos (undergraduate economics examination), and
- The best dissertation in the same part of the course.

Adam Smith, the author of *Lectures on Jurisprudence* (and also *The Wealth of Nations* and *The Theory of Moral Sentiments*), was a Scottish philosopher and economist, and the prizes are named in his honor.
